question: [File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a configuration where `scenarios` is populated but every `PublishScenarioProperties` has `recordPercent = 0.0`, causing the monitor to publish transactions without ever requesting records from mirror nodes, resulting in zero records exported and no detection of missing records?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a scenario map where scenario names contain only whitespace characters that pass the `StringUtils.isBlank` check inconsistently, causing `property.setName(name)` to assign a blank name and silently corrupt scenario identity in exported records?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user set `enabled = true` with a single scenario where `tps = 0.0`, causing `CompositeTransactionGenerator.rebuild()` to set `rateLimiter` to `INACTIVE_RATE_LIMITER` and silently stop all transaction publishing, resulting in no records ever being exported to mirror nodes?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user configure `warmupPeriod` to its maximum allowed value (no upper bound constraint), causing the `RateLimiter` warmup to throttle all transactions for an extended period and produce missing records in the mirror node export window?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user set `batchDivisor` to exactly its minimum value of 100 while configuring a very high aggregate `tps`, causing `batchSize` to be computed as a large integer that overwhelms the rate limiter and causes transactions to be dropped before records are exported?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user set `async = false` in `PublishProperties` while configuring a very short `timeout` in `PublishScenarioProperties`, causing synchronous `execute()` calls to time out before the record query completes, resulting in missing records exported to mirror nodes?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user configure `responseThreads = 1` (minimum allowed) while setting a high `tps`, causing a thread starvation condition where record responses are never processed and records are silently missing from mirror node exports?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user configure `nodeMaxBackoff` to `Duration.ofMinutes(1)` (default) combined with a flaky node, causing the SDK to back off and retry on a different node, resulting in records being submitted to a node that does not export them to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user set `statusFrequency` to its minimum value of 1 second, causing excessive status logging that floods the log output and masks error messages about missing records exported to mirror nodes?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user configure `clients = 1` (minimum) so all transactions go through a single SDK client, creating a single point of failure where if that client loses its connection, all subsequent records are missing from mirror node exports?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user configure `scenarios` with duplicate logical scenario names that differ only in case (e.g., `Scenario1` vs `scenario1`), causing `property.setName(name)` to assign conflicting names and produce incorrect scenario attribution in exported records?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user set `recordPercent = 0.0` and `receiptPercent = 0.0` in all scenarios, causing `processTransactionResponse()` to always return `Mono.just(builder)` without querying for records or receipts, resulting in zero records exported to mirror nodes while the monitor reports success?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user configure `warmupPeriod = Duration.ZERO` (allowed by `@DurationMin(seconds = 0)`), causing `getRateLimiter()` to skip warmup and immediately publish at full rate before mirror nodes are ready to ingest, resulting in missing records?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user configure a scenario with `timeout` set to exactly 1 second (minimum), causing `TransactionPublisher.publish()` to time out before the `TransactionRecordQuery` completes, resulting in records being submitted but never confirmed as exported to mirror nodes?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user configure `maxMemoLength = 13` (minimum), causing `StringUtils.truncate(memoMessage, 13)` to truncate the memo to only the timestamp portion, stripping the scenario name from the memo and making it impossible to correlate exported records with their originating scenario?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user configure `enabled = false` after the application starts (via config refresh), causing `TransactionPublisher.publish()` to return `Mono.empty()` and silently stop all record exports without any alerting mechanism?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user configure a scenario with `limit = 1` (via `ScenarioProperties`), causing `ConfigurableTransactionGenerator.next()` to throw `ScenarioException` after a single transaction, disabling the scenario and resulting in missing records for all subsequent expected exports?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user configure `async = true` (default) and a very short `timeout`, causing `Mono.fromFuture(executable.executeAsync(client))` to be cancelled by the timeout operator before the future completes, resulting in transactions submitted to the network but records never retrieved or verified as exported?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user configure `scenarios` as a `LinkedHashMap` with a very large number of entries (e.g., thousands), causing `validate()` to iterate all entries and `scenarios.forEach((name, property) -> property.setName(name))` to set names in insertion order, but the `EnumeratedDistribution` sampling in `CompositeTransactionGenerator` to produce skewed record exports that miss certain scenario types?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user configure `nodeMaxBackoff` to `Duration.ZERO` (no minimum constraint), causing the SDK client to retry failed node connections with zero backoff, flooding the network with duplicate transactions and producing incorrect duplicate records exported to mirror nodes?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user configure `batchDivisor` at exactly 100 (minimum) with `tps = 0.001` across all scenarios, causing `batchSize = Math.max(1, (int) Math.ceil(0.001 / 100))` to always be 1, but the rate limiter to throttle so aggressively that records are exported far below the expected rate, causing mirror node gap detection to fail?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user configure `logResponse = true` in a scenario, causing `TransactionPublisher.publish()` to log every response at INFO level, and if the logging infrastructure is compromised or slow, cause back-pressure that delays record processing and results in missing records in mirror node exports?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user configure `receiptPercent = 1.0` but `recordPercent = 0.0`, causing the monitor to only verify receipts (not full records) via `TransactionReceiptQuery`, and since receipts do not contain the full record data, mirror node record export completeness is never verified?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user configure `recordPercent = 1.0` but set `timeout` to 1 second (minimum), causing `TransactionRecordQuery` with a fixed `queryPayment` of 1 HBAR to time out before the record is returned, resulting in records submitted but never confirmed as exported to mirror nodes?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/PublishProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user configure `clients` to a very large value (no upper bound constraint), causing `getClients()` to create an excessive number of SDK clients that exhaust connection pool resources, leading to failed record queries and missing records in mirror node exports?
